source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 9.6.2-9.6.6 background jobs (pp. 163-165)

questions.q: What does nohup let you do?
options: Launch a process independently of the connection - it ignores the SIGHUP sent at logout
Kill a process by name
Raise a process's priority
List background jobs
questions.why: nohup makes a process ignore SIGHUP (sent when the user logs out), so it keeps running after you disconnect.

questions.q: Why is nohup often used with '0</dev/null'?
options: nohup handles standard output and error but not standard input, so input is redirected from /dev/null
To discard the program's output
To speed up the process
Because nohup cannot write to files
questions.why: nohup covers stdout and stderr but not stdin, so stdin is redirected from /dev/null.

questions.q: What does appending '&' to a command do?
options: Runs it asynchronously (in the background) as a job and returns the prompt immediately, showing [job] PID
Runs it with the highest priority
Runs it as root
Redirects its output to a file
questions.why: & backgrounds the command (now a 'job'), printing the job number in brackets and the PID, and gives the prompt back.

questions.q: What does pressing Ctrl+z do to a running foreground process?
options: Temporarily suspends it and returns the prompt (showing the suspended job number)
Kills it immediately
Sends it to the background and keeps it running
Raises its priority
questions.why: Ctrl+z suspends the synchronous process (SIGSTOP); you can then resume it in the background with bg.

questions.q: Bring background job number 1 to the FOREGROUND.
questions.why: fg <job> brings that job to the foreground; bg <job> resumes it in the background.

questions.q: List the jobs currently running in the background, with their job numbers.
questions.why: jobs lists background/suspended jobs and their numbers, states and commands.

questions.q: In the 'jobs' output, what does the + marker mean?
options: It is the default job for fg/bg when no job number is given
It is a stopped job
It is a job owned by root
It is the oldest job
questions.why: The + job is the default target of fg/bg; the - job is the next one in line to become +.
